strategy_criteria_summary: USA-listed companies reporting earnings next week, sorted by market cap. This deliberately broad Finviz screen preserves the top five visible rows; optionability, liquidity, trend, news, fundamentals, and contract quality are validated downstream.
scoring_reasons: trend alignment was clearly supportive|market/sector environment was clearly supportive|price structure was clearly supportive|best contract was long_call 2026-05-15 100|IV is elevated for a long option.
decision_reasoning: USAR offers the cleanest directional setup: +87% over 20d, +46% over 50d, RS meaningfully above both SPY and sector, volume 1.3x 20d average, and data confidence 98. The 27.5 strike expiring two days after earnings is the best expiry fit in the bundle (BMO/AMC rule) with liquid volume (1,709) and a manageable 14.9% spread. IV at 113% is the main drag — post-earnings crush is a real risk — which is why this lands as a Standard rather than Strong recommend. CSCO's strike is too far OTM with low delta; RMBS has mixed news and a deep OTM 135 strike; LUNR has flatter short-term momentum and similar IV risk without better structure.
